frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: trial-and-error policy learning over latent neural states defined in real-time by fMRI

frameworks.name: metacognition / decision confidence
frameworks.one_line: confidence meaningfulness gates exploration of high-dimensional latent task representations

frameworks.name: frontostriatal reward prediction error
frameworks.one_line: prefrontal confidence synchronizes with basal-ganglia RPE to support latent-state learning

frameworks.name: curse of dimensionality
frameworks.one_line: framed as the computational challenge the brain must overcome during unconscious learning

core_question: Can humans learn reward-maximizing policies defined over unconscious, high-dimensional latent neural states?
key_assumptions: reward contingencies can be tied to multivoxel fMRI patterns in real time
the relevant brain states remain below conscious threshold by design
decision confidence carries computationally meaningful information about latent states
manipulations: trial-by-trial choices yielding reinforcement signals over hidden states
key_findings: subjects solve the hidden-state task within ~200 trials
RL processes interact with metacognitive confidence meaningfulness
prefrontal confidence representations synchronize with striatal reward prediction errors
interpretations: frontostriatal confidence–RPE coupling lets the brain explore latent task structure despite high dimensionality
metacognition provides a route to unconscious learning of hidden representations
